node.title: Uber | Phone screen | Serialize and Deserialize an N-ary Tree

topic.title: Uber | Phone screen | Serialize and Deserialize an N-ary Tree

post.content:
[@dd2233](https://leetcode.com/dd2233 "User") 
I think it is acceptable to make this assumption for an integer-type node during an interview. 
Also, it is an interesting follow-up to deal with a different data type. Take the string-type node as an example. My idea is to assign index for every node, and use an array to store every corresponding string.
```
       apple
     /   |   \
   book  cat  dog
  / | \       / \
 e  f  g     h   i
```
can be transformed into
```
         0
     /   |   \
    1    2    3
  / | \      / \
 4  5  6    7   8

["apple", "book", "cat", "dog", "e", "f", "g", h", "i"]

```

I like your answer, OP, but since a tree is a recursively defined data-type, I think a nice serialization could be done recursively.  Something like

```
serialize(root):
    print start node symbol
    print root value
    print root number of children and begin children symbol
    for each child of root:
        serialize(child)
    print end children symbol
    print end node symbol
```

I actually like this answer. The alternate solution is to print out the tree as is (without printing/storing the number of children) and use some character to represent null, but when N is large we would potentially need a lot of those null characters

Was it specified that the value stored in each node is just a number?\n\nIf not, what happens to your serialization if the value stored in each node is, in fact, an entire N-Ary tree which stores integers?
your solution is good it is same as leetcode tree serlization.  You could have used queue as datastructure. I guess you are replicating same via List
